title: Iran Rejects Trump Peace Overture, Military Declares Readiness for Resumed Conflict
summary: Iran's military declared it stands 'fully prepared for any new adventures' from the US as Trump rejected Tehran's latest peace proposal, raising the prospect of renewed hostilities after the April 8 ceasefire.
contents:
## Iran-US Diplomatic Standoff Intensifies

### Latest Developments
Iranian state news agency IRNA reported that Tehran sent its latest negotiation proposal to Pakistani mediators on May 2, 2026. President Trump swiftly rejected the offer, stating Iran requested concessions he "can't agree to."

### Military Posture
Iran's military issued a statement declaring it considers resumption of the US-Israel war "likely" and stands "fully prepared for any new adventures or foolishness from the Americans." This represents a significant hardening of rhetoric compared to the April 8 ceasefire period.

### Sanctions and Enforcement Context
The ongoing naval blockade of Iranian oil exports continues despite the ceasefire, creating a gray zone between war and peace. Congressional Democrats argue the blockade constitutes continuing hostilities under the War Powers Resolution.

### Economic Warfare Dimensions
The conflict has severely disrupted global energy markets. Iran previously closed the Strait of Hormuz, triggering worldwide oil price spikes. While the strait has reopened, the US blockade continues to target Iranian oil shipments globally, pursuing Iranian-linked vessels across multiple oceans.

### Implications for Nuclear Program
The 40-day war destroyed significant Iranian military infrastructure but left nuclear facilities largely intact. The IAEA Director General recently confirmed continued nuclear activities, and the massive UXO contamination across Iran complicates post-conflict assessment of nuclear site damage.
